title: Mustardy Uruguayan Eggs Frittata
description: A twist on the classic Uruguayan dish, this mustardy eggs frittata is a great kid-friendly breakfast option with fresh herbs.
instructions: Break the eggs into a large bowl and whisk them together with a fork until the whites and yolks are fully incorporated.
Heat the olive oil in a 10-inch non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 4-5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for another minute.
Add the diced carrot and continue cooking until the vegetables are soft, about 4-5 minutes.
Add the diced bell peppers and sauté for another 4-5 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes and cook until the mixture is heated through.
Pour in the whisked eggs and cook until the edges start to set, about 2-3 minutes.
Sprinkle the shredded cheddar cheese on top of the eggs and transfer the skillet to the oven.
Bake the frittata at 375°F (190°C) for 15-20 minutes, or until the eggs are set and the cheese is melted and golden brown.
Remove the frittata from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Slice the frittata into wedges and serve hot or cold, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
Sprinkle a pinch of salt and black pepper to taste.
If desired, serve with a side of toast or a salad for a more filling meal.
If you want to make this recipe vegan, omit the cheese and use a dairy-free alternative or nutritional yeast for Extra cheesy flavor.
